spec_match.rationale: The page now uses the spec's `container` PerformanceObserver entry type and surfaces identifier, timing, size, rootElement, lastPaintedElement, and ignored-subtree behavior.

interactive.rationale: Replay, ignore-subtree, and observer-probe controls update the article timing waterfall and live entry logs.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature-index blurb and page lede both describe section-level paint measurement via `containertiming` and PerformanceObserver.

edge_cases.rationale: The page explicitly detects unsupported browsers, shows a simulation fallback, probes the legacy wrong entry type, and lets users compare included versus ignored subtree sizes.

references.rationale: The see-also section links to the valid ChromeStatus entry at https://chromestatus.com/feature/5110962817073152.

design_system.rationale: The new controls use CSS variables, set button background and foreground together, and replace the static-card pattern with live readouts.

summary: Converted the blog-post concept into an interactive Container Timing lab, corrected the observer entry type and ChromeStatus ID, and added graceful fallback plus ignored-subtree probes.
